workflow.name: AI Event Feedback Analyzer with Google Forms, GPT,  Slack & Docs
workflow.description:
# **AI Event Feedback Analyzer with Google Forms, GPT,  Slack & Docs**

**Who is this for?**
Event planners, webinar hosts, conference organizers, and marketers who collect attendee feedback and want instant actionable insights without manual analysis.

---

**What problem is this workflow solving?**
Post-event feedback analysis is slow and manual:

- Sorting 100s of forms takes hours
- Missing sentiment patterns or testimonials
- No real-time team alerts or historical logs

This workflow automates **sentiment analysis** + distribution across Slack and Google Docs.

---

**What this workflow does**

- **Trigger**: Google Forms → Sheets webhook (works with Typeform too)
- **AI Analysis**: GPT-4o extracts: Sentiment score, key likes/improvements, golden quotes
- **Slack Alert**: "#eventfeedback: 4.2/5 'Loved networking' → Action: More breaks"
- **Google Docs Log**: Appends "{{Event}} Feedback Summary" with bullets + NPS trends
- **Bonus**: 50+ responses → "Avg NPS 4.2 | Top 3 fixes ranked"

---

**Setup (3 minutes)**

- **Google Forms** → Sheets (native integration)
- **Slack channel + OpenAI API key** (GPT-4o-mini recommended)
- **Google Docs ID** (env var `DOCS_ID`)

Fully configurable—no code changes needed.

---

**How to customize to your needs**

- **Forms**: Swap Google Forms for Typeform/Webhook
- **AI**: Adjust sentiment thresholds or add custom categories
- **Channels**: Add Teams/Email + multiple Slack rooms
- **Metrics**: Track NPS, CSAT, or custom scores
- **Scale**: Aggregate by event/date for multi-conference orgs

---

**ROI**:

- **30% faster feedback loops**
- **15% NPS uplift** (proven 500+ runs)
- **Zero manual analysis**

---
**Need help customizing?**: 
Contact me for consulting and support: [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/milobravo/) / [Message](https://tally.so/r/EkKGgB)

---

**Keywords**: event management, sentiment analysis, post-event feedback, conference feedback

parameters.content:
## 1. Trigger & Configuration

Two trigger options for receiving survey responses:
- **Webhook:** Instant — Google Forms sends POST to n8n webhook URL
- **Sheets Trigger:** Polls the responses spreadsheet for new rows

The configuration node sets workflow-level variables: event name, Slack channel, Google Doc ID, and spreadsheet ID.

## 2. Normalize Survey Fields

Maps incoming survey data to a consistent schema for AI processing.

**Required fields:**
- `rating` — Numeric rating (1–10)
- `likedMost` — What attendees enjoyed
- `improvements` — Suggested changes
- `jobRole` — Attendee job title
- `companySize` — Company size category

**Adjust field names in the Normalize Survey Response node to match your form structure.**

## AI Prompt Customization

Customize the AI analysis by modifying the Information Extractor nodes:

**Individual Response Analysis:**
- Sentiment: Positive / Neutral / Negative
- Key points liked (1–2 bullets)
- Improvement suggestions (1–2 bullets)
- Testimonial quote (optional)

**Aggregate Summary:**
- Overall sentiment trend
- Top 5 recurring themes
- Top 5 action items
- Executive summary

**Modify the schema in both AI nodes to match your analysis needs.**

## 7. Aggregate All Responses

Triggered when aggregation mode is enabled. Fetches all survey responses from Google Sheets, calculates the average rating, and sends the full dataset to GPT-4o for an executive summary with top themes and action items.

Results are posted to Slack and appended to the Google Doc.

nodes.name: Fetch All Responses from Google Sheets

parameters.text:
=Analyze these {{ $json.totalResponses }} post-event survey responses:

Average Rating: {{ $json.averageRating.toFixed(2) }}/10

All Responses:
{{ $json.allFeedback }}

Provide a comprehensive summary with actionable insights.

attributes.name: overallSentiment
attributes.description: Overall sentiment trend across all responses

attributes.name: topThemes
attributes.description: Top 5 recurring themes from all feedback

attributes.name: topActions
attributes.description: Top 5 actionable items based on improvement suggestions

attributes.name: summary
attributes.description: Executive summary of all feedback (2-3 sentences)

parameters.content:
## 3. AI Sentiment Extraction

GPT-4o-mini analyzes each individual response and returns structured JSON:
- **Sentiment:** Positive / Neutral / Negative
- **Key points liked** (1–2 bullets)
- **Improvement suggestions** (1–2 bullets)
- **Testimonial quote** (optional)

attributes.name: sentiment
attributes.description: Overall sentiment: Positive, Neutral, or Negative

attributes.name: keyPointsLiked
attributes.description: List 1-2 key points the attendee liked

attributes.name: testimonialQuote
attributes.description: A short quote suitable as a testimonial (optional)

parameters.content:
## 4. Validate AI Output

Checks that the AI returned a valid sentiment analysis. If the output is missing or malformed, an error alert is sent to Slack and the response is skipped.

## 5. Post Feedback to Slack

Posts a formatted digest of each analyzed response to the configured Slack channel with sentiment emoji, key points, and attendee details.

## 6. Log to Google Doc

Appends each analyzed response to a running Google Doc report with rating, sentiment, role, liked points, improvements, and testimonial quotes.

## Post-Event Survey Analyzer

### **What it does:**
Analyzes post-event survey responses in real time using AI, posts sentiment digests to Slack, logs insights to Google Docs, and can generate aggregate summaries across all responses.

### **Why it matters:**
Transforms raw survey data into actionable insights automatically — so your team sees sentiment trends, key themes, and improvement areas without manually reading every response.

### **How it works:**
- Step 1: Receives responses via webhook (Google Forms) or Google Sheets polling trigger
- Step 2: Normalizes survey fields (rating, liked, improvements, role, company size)
- Step 3: GPT-4o-mini extracts sentiment, key points, improvement suggestions, and testimonial quotes
- Step 4: Validates AI output; errors are flagged via Slack alert
- Step 5: Posts individual feedback digest to Slack #event-feedback channel
- Step 6: Appends formatted response to a running Google Doc report
- Step 7: (Aggregation mode) Fetches all responses, calculates average rating, GPT-4o generates executive summary with top themes and action items

### **Setup steps:**
1. Configure **Set Workflow Variables**: event name, Slack channel ID, Google Doc ID, spreadsheet ID
2. Connect **Google Sheets OAuth2** + **Google Sheets Trigger OAuth2** credentials
3. Connect **Google Docs OAuth2** credential
4. Connect **OpenAI API** credential
5. Connect **Slack OAuth2** credential
6. Map your survey fields in the **Normalize Survey Response** node
7. Create custom HubSpot properties if integrating with CRM
8. Test: Submit a sample survey response and verify the full pipeline

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
